original: Bornanew spares you a single rueful look.

On a pedestal of carved wood, in a spot of light like holy revelation, a single glove is upthrust. Its fingers appear frozen in the midst of just starting to curl back toward a relaxed state after giving you a little wave.

"Do you know what this is?" Menes whispers to you. 

"It looks like a glove, demarch," Bornanew answers in feigned misunderstanding, stepping forward.

"Not any glove," Menes says with more than a hint of vexation.

He begins again in a reverent whisper, "The glove worn by Ludd upon stepping upon the sacred Earth of Gilead. Ludd removed the glove and kissed the ground, according to the Luddic religion. Isn't that right, sir Knight? And this... is it."

Bornanew looks at you, his eyebrows rising. "Could it be?" he asks, allowing some melodrama to enter his voice.

"Precisely my reaction upon first hearing word of this unique artifact!" Menes beams, more than a little pleased that someone appreciates his trinket.

"Setting aside its purely religious significance," he gives a glance at Bornanew, "it represents a, a heralding of the changing tides of history! The ebbing influence of the Domain-era megacorporations, the rising of what some call - not my words of course, sir Knight - an atavistic populism. Truly, it is this small piece of the vastness of the story of Humanity which called to me when it was first described to me..."

Menes seems prepared to continue this for quite some time.
Menes is blathering on about something or other while Bornanew pretends to listen.

This isn't working. It's a good thing you prepared a backup plan.

You'll simply call in a marine raid while keeping Menes distracted. You give a grim nod to Bornanew and with only a small pause he follows your lead, asking Menes an insightful question about another item in his collection.

You glance at your comms. Everything has been planned ahead of time, and care taken to hide tactical teams among the general population of offworld crew taking a bit of shore-leave. Signal discipline has been unerring.

The coded response, hidden in routine status reports, shows all teams are prepped and ready to move.
